title: Build the Founder Thesis Lab
status_reason: The PT3 product implementation and every PT3 acceptance criterion are covered, but Gate B truthfully remains INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE because no real founder task corpus or paired founder trials were supplied. Synthetic fixtures are excluded by contract.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Real code tasks can be packaged reproducibly.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: The CLI capture/freeze/export test restores a content-addressed Git snapshot, validates it, freezes it, and verifies that the portable archive contains actual allowed code and no evaluator-only material.
Paired runner tests assert the same immutable and restored digest for direct and Villani arms after the source repository changes.
Runner payload and portable bundle tests exclude task identity, future context, hidden checks, expected patch facts, arm, route, harness, cost, and competing candidates.
The blinded queue and append-only villani.human_review.v1 ledger preserve amendments, correction details, rollback/reopened facts, review time, severity, false acceptance, and false rejection.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Reliability, cost, time, and supervision burden are measured.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Metric tests cover rates, raw counts, Wilson intervals, median bootstrap intervals, review time, attempts, escalation, disagreement, exclusions, paired deltas, unknown cost, and mixed-currency refusal.
The evaluation workflow only runs explicit commands; suite validation reports passive_monitoring=false and CLI initialization states that passive monitoring is disabled.
The direct arm reuses VillaniCodeRunner once and the Villani arm reuses the existing public product CLI. Suite validation reports external_harness=false.
No PT4 qualification, team, SaaS, learned routing, or later-milestone behavior was introduced.

architecture_and_product_decisions: Five strict Pydantic contracts generate the normative and packaged JSON Schemas and have matching TypeScript models. Existing run-bundle contracts are untouched.

Baselines are exact Git commits captured as deterministic regular-file ZIP archives with path safety, executable-mode preservation, built-in/operator exclusions, high-confidence secret rejection, archive hashing, and byte-for-byte restore proof.

Future context and hidden checks live under evaluator-only storage. The only runner payload omits task identity, future/expected solution facts, evaluator material, arm, route, harness, cost, and competing candidates; portable exports use opaque task slots.

The direct arm chooses the strongest enabled coding backend, invokes VillaniCodeRunner exactly once, receives the same runner-visible task/criteria/validation context, and gets no Villani retry, selection, or corrective verifier feedback.

The Villani arm invokes the normal performance-mode public product path with mandatory verification and non-delivery approval. Both arms restore fresh isolation and never mutate the source repository.
A second fresh baseline and one arm-blind authoritative verifier evaluate both patches. Verification inputs contain no arm, harness, route, cost, or competing-candidate identity.

A persisted deterministic randomized plan and atomic suite lock prevent duplicates. Completed/excluded trials skip on resume; interrupted identities resume; a hard-crash stale lock fails closed with one exact removal action.

Cost values come only from captured provider telemetry/configured billing facts and optional measured local watts/runtime/electricity price. Unknown stays null; incompatible currencies are never combined or compared.
Human labels are a separate append-only JSONL ledger. Amendments name an earlier record and never rewrite it. The review queue hides arm identity where feasible.

Reports include raw counts, Wilson proportion intervals, deterministic median bootstrap intervals, paired deltas, confusion matrix, precision/recall/specificity/F1 where defined, undefined calibration rather than a fabricated probability, cost/route decomposition, wrong cases, and trial links.
Gate B is conservative: synthetic evidence is ineligible and insufficient volume never creates a significance claim.

schema_and_configuration_migrations.compatibility: New content-addressed suite metadata; no existing configuration or run bundle changes.
New frozen task/snapshot/evaluator contract; runner-safe projection is explicit and existing artifacts remain readable.
New paired-trial artifact with truthful unknown accounting and full identity metadata.
New append-only human label record; it does not overwrite trial or verifier judgments.
New confidentiality-marked report contract; no existing report schema changed.
Evaluation workspaces are opt-in directories. Existing backend, policy, delivery, and run configuration remain unchanged.

tests_added_or_extended: 15 Python PT3 tests cover immutable capture, same-baseline pairing, deterministic randomized order, interrupted resume, concurrent duplicate lockout, direct-arm isolation, evaluator/future non-leakage, portable actual-code export, secret exclusion, secret patch rejection, setup contamination, append-only review, metric/accounting correctness, all Gate states, report structure/redaction, cross-platform external paths, CLI workflow, no passive monitoring, no external harness, and strongest-backend selection.
Two TypeScript tests cover all five exact schema identities, evaluator-only expected-patch absence, and explicit unknown cost units.
Shared protocol tests now validate all five fixtures, root/package schema parity, semantic parsing, and a total of 18 v1 protocol schemas.

mandatory_test_mapping.direct_arm_isolation: one call per arm assertion and strongest configured backend test

PASS: no whitespace errors; existing LF-to-CRLF working-copy warnings only.

artifacts: Five normative and five packaged JSON Schemas.
CLI capture/freeze/export smoke artifact generated and inspected by test.
JSON, Markdown, and linked HTML evaluation reports generated and inspected by test.
end_to_end_artifacts_and_screenshots.screenshot_reason: PT3 adds a CLI/evidence workflow, not a new browser surface; no screenshot contract was requested.

known_failures_and_skips: Gate B is INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: 0 of 30 required paired real founder tasks and 0 of 2 required repositories are available. No synthetic evidence was counted.
The untouched Villani Code full suite has 74 baseline failures, 612 passes, and 1 opt-in Claude Code smoke skip. PT3 does not modify Villani Code and does not hide this failure.

Villani Ops skips two operating-system capability tests because host Python supports neither Unix-domain sockets nor FIFO creation; 114 slow/integration/e2e tests are deselected by the component's declared default pytest markers.
Broad followed-import mypy has 206 existing transitive errors. The complete direct PT3 module is clean under focused mypy.
The standalone Run Model checkout lacks local npm executables; the pinned repository TypeScript, Vitest, and Prettier binaries pass build, no-emit typecheck, 7 tests, and formatting.
security_privacy_data_loss_compatibility: High-confidence secret scanning and output redaction reduce accidental disclosure but cannot recognize every secret format. Operators must use exclusions and inspect portable bundles before sharing.

Portable bundles contain actual source code and reports disclose agent/model/provider metadata. The confidentiality marker is advisory; artifacts are local files and are not encrypted by this milestone.

Allowed setup, coding systems, and authoritative validation execute processes with the configured provider's permissions. Fresh Git isolation is not a kernel sandbox; hostile tasks should use an existing container/devcontainer execution provider.

The source repository is read only during capture and never receives a trial patch. Evaluation workspaces, review ledgers, reports, and trial bundles are new local data; no delete or migration occurs.
A hard-killed process may leave evaluation-run.lock. Execution then fails closed and prints the exact manual removal action rather than risking duplicate trials.
Blinding is feasible rather than cryptographic: reviewers must not open run-plan.json or trial.json before labeling, as documented.
All five contracts are additive. Existing run bundles, configurations, acceptance, routing, selection, verification, and delivery behavior remain readable and unchanged.

user_facing_behavior.before: Villani had offline capability evaluation and replay evidence, but no reproducible real-task suite capture, paired direct-versus-product runner, blinded review ledger, thesis metrics/report, or Founder Gate command.
user_facing_behavior.after: `villani eval` initializes a local suite, imports/restores an immutable commit, captures and validates tasks, freezes and exports runner-safe actual code, runs randomized resumable paired trials, queues blinded review, appends labels/amendments, generates JSON/Markdown/HTML reports, and returns PASS/FAIL/INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE for Gate B.

assumptions: No founder task corpus, trial credentials, measured task outcomes, or authorization to incur provider cost was supplied; the implementation therefore stops before any real paid trial.
Authoritative validation commands are supplied by the suite author and are appropriate for each task; the lab never invents hidden checks or expected solutions.
One configured process runs a suite at a time; the atomic lock rejects concurrent processes and gives a recovery action for a stale lock.
